section_miscategorizations.text: Order shoulder radiographs to evaluate for rotator cuff tendinopathy.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The rest of the note concerns abdominal pain, urinary burning, renal and gynecologic findings; introducing a new shoulder-focused diagnostic question (rotator cuff tendinopathy) without any supporting history or findings is incongruent and suggests this statement belongs to a different clinical problem/assessment, making its presence here misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: Initiate physical therapy to address muscle weakness and improve range of motion.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This plan targets musculoskeletal issues (muscle weakness, limited range of motion) that are not described anywhere in the HPI or Findings for this visit, implying a different underlying assessment/problem and creating a misleading mismatch with the documented clinical picture.

section_miscategorizations.text: Continue acetaminophen as needed for pain relief.
section_miscategorizations.reason: While analgesia can be a reasonable symptomatic plan, in this context it appears to be part of an unrelated musculoskeletal treatment bundle (with shoulder imaging and PT) rather than clearly tied to the documented abdominal/urinary complaint, which may confuse the reader about the targeted problem.

section_miscategorizations.text: Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to assess progress and adjust treatment as necessary.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The follow-up is framed around monitoring progress of treatments (radiographs, PT) that are not aligned with the documented abdominal/urinary problem, creating organizational confusion about what condition is being followed, though it remains structurally a plan item.
